one_time_description: Large earnings from Government Business Entities (primarily Kvamua Enterprises LP) of $12,234,828 vs $1,493,484 prior year, driven by significant increase in Kvamua partners' capital; also $2,762,381 in remediation costs and $9,062,841 in donations and financial support recorded as expenses
economic_notes: Nation holds interests in Kvamua Enterprises Limited Partnership (100%), Central Coast Commercial Fishing LP (25%), Central Coast Commercial Fisheries Corporation (25%), Wuikinuxv Economic Development Corporation, Great Bear Carbon Credit Limited Partnership, Great Bear Carbon Credit Corporation, and BC First Nations Gaming LP. Kvamua revenues were $54.5M for year ended Dec 31 2021. Great Bear Carbon Credit LP generated $471,526 in revenue. Midawis Maintenance Services LP is a consolidated controlled entity.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2022 but audit report signed January 5, 2024 — very late filing. Large spike in GBE earnings ($12.2M vs $1.5M prior year) driven by Kvamua Enterprises LP equity pickup. Kvamua financials are for year ended Dec 31, 2021 (lag period). $9,062,841 in donations/financial support recorded as expense — unusual large amount. Liability for contaminated sites increased from $942,508 to $3,704,889 due to diesel spill ($2,357,802). Two CIRNAC specific claim loans received ($117,900 and $109,650). No schedule of remuneration included. Province amount in segment schedule ($11,682,202) differs from income statement ($10,444,817) due to segment aggregation. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $9,942,422 + Province of BC $10,444,817 + FNHA $1,237,385 + DFO $688,056 + CMHC $55,429 + CIRNAC $25,478 + WKNTC $75,963 + Great Bear Initiative Society $603,528 = $23,072,078; osr = $37,872,746 - $23,072,078 = $14,800,668. annual_debt_service is next year principal payment per schedule.
